raw_consumer_statement:
To Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am filing a complaint regarding a denied credit card dispute with American Express for an XXXX hotel booking. 

On XX/XX/year>, I booked a non-refundable hotel room and added insurance for {$150.00}, which stated a full refund upon cancellation. 

Shortly after booking, I canceled due to personal circumstances while traveling with my XXXX XXXX. 

During a phone call with XXXX, I explicitly confirmed more than once that I would receive a full refund. The representative confirmed this and processed the cancellation, including the insurance, which was later refunded. 

However, few days later I was charged the full hotel amount of {$2600.00}, and my refund request was denied on the basis that the insurance had been canceled. This directly contradicts what I was told during the cancellation call. 

I relied on this verbal confirmation when canceling, and was charged for a service I did not use. I have not received any documentation explaining the basis used to deny my dispute. 

I believe the investigation failed to properly consider the merchants explicit verbal commitment at the time of cancellation. 

I request a full refund and a review of the dispute outcome. 

Thank you for you consideration.
